skill.name: documentation
skill.description: Create, update, and organize project documentation with structured templates, section-level scripts, and rules that reduce duplication.
skill.summary: Maintain consistent project documentation with templates, workflows, and section-level editing helpers.
skill.author: DaoThiHuong2111

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many command, filesystem, and weak-crypto patterns, but most are false positives from Markdown code fences, path examples, and documentation templates. The confirmed risk is moderate: bundled Bash helpers can read or modify caller-supplied filesystem paths without constraining them to docs or Markdown files. No evidence found of malicious intent, credential exfiltration, live network access, or prompt injection attempts.

medium_findings.title: Unconstrained Documentation File Modification
medium_findings.description: The update, delete, and insert helpers accept caller-provided file paths, check only that files exist, and replace the target with generated temporary content. This is legitimate documentation tooling, but it can modify non-documentation files if invoked with an unsafe path.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scripts directly write back to caller-selected paths and do not enforce a docs directory or .md extension. The behavior is documented and not malicious, so the concern is misuse or accidental destructive edits.

medium_findings.title: Broad Local Filesystem Reads Through Helper Scripts
medium_findings.description: Search, metadata, and duplicate-detection helpers read caller-supplied files or directories. They are intended for documentation, but they do not restrict traversal to a documentation root and can expose local Markdown content or file metadata if used on a broad path.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem reads are visible and legitimate for a documentation skill, but their scope is entirely controlled by the caller. I found no exfiltration path, which limits severity.

low_findings.title: Markdown Static Analysis False Positives
low_findings.description: Many reported external command, weak cryptography, and path traversal hits occur inside Markdown templates, examples, or documentation paths. These are not executable code paths and mostly reflect backticks, relative links, placeholder paths, and ordinary Markdown content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review showed these locations are documentation examples and templates rather than code that executes. The scanner appears to have treated Markdown code fences and paths as active shell or filesystem behavior.

low_findings.title: Documentation URL Is Not Network Execution
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL finding is a reference link in script documentation, not code that performs an outbound request. I found no script network commands or data exfiltration behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are in a README See Also section and do not invoke curl, wget, fetch, or another network client. This is a clear static false positive for runtime network risk.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Evidence Found
low_findings.description: A targeted review for override language, fake system instructions, pre-approval claims, and requests to skip analysis found no evidence in the reviewed skill content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The main skill instructions describe normal documentation workflows and tool permissions. I also searched for common prompt injection phrases and found no matches.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unconstrained Documentation File Modification
dangerous_patterns.description: The update, delete, and insert helpers accept caller-provided file paths, check only that files exist, and replace the target with generated temporary content. This is legitimate documentation tooling, but it can modify non-documentation files if invoked with an unsafe path.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The scripts directly write back to caller-selected paths and do not enforce a docs directory or .md extension. The behavior is documented and not malicious, so the concern is misuse or accidental destructive edits.

dangerous_patterns.title: Broad Local Filesystem Reads Through Helper Scripts
dangerous_patterns.description: Search, metadata, and duplicate-detection helpers read caller-supplied files or directories. They are intended for documentation, but they do not restrict traversal to a documentation root and can expose local Markdown content or file metadata if used on a broad path.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem reads are visible and legitimate for a documentation skill, but their scope is entirely controlled by the caller. I found no exfiltration path, which limits severity.

content.value_statement: Project documentation can become scattered, duplicated, and difficult to maintain. This skill provides templates, workflows, and helper scripts for consistent Markdown documentation.
actual_capabilities: Searches Markdown documentation with bundled shell helpers.
Lists headings and extracts specific sections from Markdown files.
Updates, inserts, or deletes sections in existing Markdown documents.
Provides templates for screen, guide, and architecture documentation.
Guides duplicate documentation consolidation and large file splitting.
Defines documentation rules for structure, line limits, links, and examples.
limitations: Assumes projects use docs, guides, architecture, and screens folders.
Requires Bash and local filesystem access for helper scripts.
Does not automatically verify whether examples still run correctly.
Helper scripts do not restrict paths to documentation directories.

use_cases.title: Create screen documentation
use_cases.description: Use screen templates to document overview, technical details, features, and user flows.

use_cases.title: Maintain architecture docs
use_cases.description: Organize architecture notes with consistent sections for components, patterns, decisions, and trade-offs.

use_cases.title: Reduce documentation duplication
use_cases.description: Find repeated sections, compare similar files, and consolidate content into a clearer source of truth.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic guide
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Markdown guide for this feature. Check for existing documentation first, then use the project documentation structure.

prompt_templates.title: Update an existing section
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the existing documentation for this topic, list its sections, and update only the relevant section with the new behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Document a screen workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create or update screen documentation for this interface, including overview, technical details, and the main user flows.

prompt_templates.title: Consolidate duplicate docs
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit the documentation for duplicate coverage of this topic, identify the canonical file, and propose a consolidation plan before editing.

output_examples.input: Create documentation for a new settings screen.
output_examples.output: A screen documentation set with an overview, technical details, feature notes, related links, and a validation checklist.

output_examples.input: Update the authentication guide after a code change.
output_examples.output: A targeted guide update that changes the affected section, preserves existing structure, and adds traceable code references.

output_examples.input: Find duplicate documentation about deployment setup.
output_examples.output: A short duplicate report that lists overlapping files, recommends one canonical location, and describes the merge steps.

best_practices: Search for existing documentation before creating a new file.
Keep each document focused and below the recommended line limit.
Ask before deleting or consolidating content that may still be useful.
anti_patterns: Creating a new document when a matching document already exists.
Running edit scripts against paths outside the documentation tree.
Adding examples without checking that they match current behavior.

faq.question: What does this skill help document?
faq.answer: It helps document screens, guides, architecture decisions, features, workflows, and technical implementation details.

faq.question: Does it write documentation automatically?
faq.answer: It can draft and edit Markdown, but it expects the agent to inspect existing files and follow the included rules.

faq.question: Can it update only one section of a file?
faq.answer: Yes. The bundled scripts can list headings, extract one section, and replace or insert section content.

faq.question: Does it require command execution?
faq.answer: Some workflows use Bash scripts for search and section edits. Manual Markdown editing is still possible.

faq.question: Is it safe for large repositories?
faq.answer: It is useful for large documentation sets, but script paths should be reviewed before execution.

faq.question: Does it support Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code compatible workflows.
